rulings.Case Title: ROSE TSAI VS. JIMMY CHAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Leave To Augment Expert Witness Disclosures
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Friday, June 14, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiffs ROSE TSAI and DAVID TSAI'S Motion for Leave to Augment Expert Witness Disclosures Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. In this case the trial date is June 24, 2013. Plaintiff has made a Motion for Leave to Augment Expert Witness Disclosures. At this late date such a motion is inappropriate and untimely. There are nevertheless some arguably extenuating circumstances (withdrawal of her former counsel on April 4, 2013, and her new counsel's desire to retain mostly new and different expert witnesses) that would warrant the granting of the motion if the trial date were to be continued. It is not within the authority of the Pro Tem Judge to continue the trial date. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SALMA KHAN MD VS. HEALTH SYSTEMS DEPARTMENT, SAN MATEO COUNTY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Friday, June 14, 2013, Line 2: Defendants COUNTY OF SAN MATEO and CELIA MORENO, M.D.'S Motion for Protective Order and Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' motion for protective order is granted. Defendant's motion for sanctions is granted, but only in the amount of $750. Plaintiff is pro per and defendant made every reasonable effort to accommodate her requests for changes in plaintiff's expert witnesses, which did not comply with applicable statutory requirements. Defendant did not reciprocate plaintiff's efforts. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 14, 2013, Line 19, Plaintiff'S (Joinder) Motion To Sever Cross-Complaint The motion to sever is denied. By its order on May 28, 2013, the Court has already granted the relief the moving party is requesting by bifurcating the cross-complaint for indemnity for trial. The underlying liability trial will go first. If the duty to indemnify becomes an issue, the trial of Menaged's cross-complaint will proceed. This second trial, if any, will not follow immediately at the conclusion of the underlying liability trial. rulings.Case Title: KASOWITZ, BENSON, TORRES & FRIEDMAN, LLP A NEW VS. JOAN MACK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 14, 2013, Line 10, CROSS COMPLAINANT LANDON MACK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendant's separate statement fails to comply with Cal. Rules of Court 3.1350(d). It does not "separately identify each cause of action, claim, issue of duty, or affirmative defense, and each supporting material fact claimed to be without dispute with respect to the cause of action ¿" Plaintiff's objections to the Kaufman Declaration are sustained in their entirety. Without the Kaufman Declaration and attached exhibits, the motion fails to shift the burden. Even if the Court were to overlook the procedural deficiencies, overrule the objections, and consider all of the evidence presented with the motion, the motion must still be denied. There are triable issues of fact regarding whether Defendant is personally liable to Plaintiff for the fees incurred under the Retainer Agreement. (See Topel Decl. 6-7, 9-13 and Exs. C, D, E, and F.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with CCP § 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: MARIYA YUKHIMOVA VS. GOLDEN STATE ADULT DAY HEALTH CARE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Oleg Scamin, Individually And Dba Scol, Co'S Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 14, 2013, Line 12, Defendant Oleg Scamin, Individually And Dba Scol, Co'S Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination Granted, in part, for good cause shown. Plaintiff's treating physician put her neurological deficits at issue in his deposition. The entire examination shall not last longer than 2 hours and will be conducted by Peter C. Cassini, M.D., a licensed neurologist at Plaintiff's residence or a mutually agreed upon location by the parties. The examination will include taking a medically relevant history. The examination will include a general physical neurologic exam of cranial nerves, motor function, dexterity and sensory function. These tests are to be done in a painless, passive manner. Depending on what is revealed in the general examination, a more focused, detailed physical examination may be conducted to better identify the source of any specific problems. The examination is not to involve any painful or protracted testing. No x-rays or any electronic or magnetic scanning will be performed during the examination. Plaintiff shall cooperate during the examination so that it may be completed within the 2 hour limitation. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: WINELAND-THOMSON ADVENTURES, INC. DBA THOMSON SAFARIS VS. DOES 1 THROUGH 10, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moton For Order Of Cotnempt And Sanctions Against Failure To Produce Documents Pursuant To Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 14, 2013, Line 17, PLAINTIFF WINELAND -THOMPSON ADVENTURES, INC'S Motion For Order Of Cotnempt And Sanctions Against Failure To Produce Documents Pursuant To Subpoena Off calendar. Discovery is stayed in light of Defendants' filing of the special motion to strike. (CCP § 425.16(g).) The stay on "[a]ll discovery proceedings" applies to discovery motions, "including those already pending at the time the special motion to strike is filed." (Britts v. Sup.Ct. (2006) 145 Cal.App.4th 1112, 1128.) Plaintiff may re-file its motion after the ruling on the special motion to strike. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: ANNETTE YIP VS. AAA FLAG & BANNER MFG. CO., INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 14, 2013, Line 18, DEFENDANT AAA FLAG & BANNER MFG. CO., INC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Continued to July 18, 2013 to give AAA Flag & Banner Mfg. Co., Inc. the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B and 2.6C. AAA Flag & Banner Mfg. Co., Inc. must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers and the operative pleading to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 14, 2013 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/MJM
